  • Axicons
GitHub

Advanced Colors & Design Tokens

Complete guide to AXIOM01's semantic color system, design tokens, and how to use them effectively in your components.

Color System Overview

AXIOM01 uses a semantic color naming system based on Material Design 3 principles. Colors are organized into functional categories rather than literal names (red, blue, green), making them meaningful and maintainable across themes.

Why Semantic Colors?
Instead of "use red for errors", we define "use error" — which adapts automatically whether your theme is light or dark, and works for users with color blindness.

Primary Color Family

The primary color is your brand color — used for the most important actions and states.

Primary
--a-color-primary
Main brand color
Primary action buttons, links, focus states
On Primary
--a-color-on-primary
Text ON primary
Text color when placed on primary backgrounds
Container
--a-color-primary-container
Light primary
Backgrounds for primary-related content, badges, cards
On Container
--a-color-on-primary-container
Text on light primary
Text when placed on primary-container backgrounds
Usage Example:
.button.primary { background: var(--a-color-primary); color: var(--a-color-on-primary); }

Secondary & Tertiary Colors

Secondary and tertiary colors provide complementary accent colors for less critical actions and supporting UI elements.

Secondary
--a-color-secondary
Secondary action
Secondary buttons, alternative actions
Secondary Container
--a-color-secondary-container
Light secondary
Secondary badges, backgrounds
Tertiary
--a-color-tertiary
Accent color
Accent elements, highlights, special actions
Tertiary Container
--a-color-tertiary-container
Light accent
Accent backgrounds

Semantic Status Colors

Status colors communicate state and feedback to users. These include success, warning, error, and info states.

Success
--a-color-success
Positive state
Successful actions, confirmations
Success Container
--a-color-success-container
Light success
Success alerts, backgrounds
Warning
--a-color-warning
Cautionary state
Warnings, caution messages
Warning Container
--a-color-warning-container
Light warning
Warning alerts, backgrounds
Error
--a-color-error
Negative state
Errors, destructive actions
Error Container
--a-color-error-container
Light error
Error alerts, backgrounds
Info
--a-color-info
Informational state
Information, hints, tips
Info Container
--a-color-info-container
Light info
Info alerts, backgrounds

Surface & Neutral Colors

Surface colors define the background hierarchy and neutral text colors for readability and contrast.

Surface
--a-color-surface
Primary background
Main background color for pages and containers
Surface Variant
--a-color-surface-variant
Secondary background
Hover states, alt backgrounds, subtle separations
On Surface
--a-color-on-surface
Primary text
Main text color on surface backgrounds
On Surface Variant
--a-color-on-surface-variant
Secondary text
Muted text, labels, helper text
Outline
--a-color-outline
Borders & dividers
Borders, lines, separators between elements

Understanding "On" Colors

The "On" prefix indicates text or foreground content that goes ON TOP of the named background color. This ensures proper contrast and readability.

Pattern: var(--a-color-on-[background])

If background is var(--a-color-primary) → text should be var(--a-color-on-primary)
If background is var(--a-color-error-container) → text should be var(--a-color-on-error-container)

Example CSS:

.button.primary {
    background-color: var(--a-color-primary);
    color: var(--a-color-on-primary);  /* Text ON this background */
}

.alert.error {
    background-color: var(--a-color-error-container);
    color: var(--a-color-on-error-container);  /* Text ON this background */
}

Understanding "Container" Colors

Container colors are lighter, tinted versions of their parent colors, used for backgrounds and less-emphasized UI elements.

Container Purpose:
var(--a-color-primary) is bold and used for buttons and actions
var(--a-color-primary-container) is subtle and used for badges, cards, and backgrounds

Example HTML/CSS:


<button class="primary">Click Me</button>


<span class="badge">New</span>
.button.primary {
    background: var(--a-color-primary);      /* Bold primary color */
}

.badge {
    background: var(--a-color-primary-container);  /* Soft, tinted version */
}

Complete Color Token Reference

Token Name Purpose Typical Use
--a-color-primary Brand color Main action buttons, important elements
--a-color-on-primary Text ON primary Text inside primary-colored elements
--a-color-primary-container Light primary Badge backgrounds, card accents
--a-color-on-primary-container Text ON container Text inside primary-container elements
--a-color-secondary Secondary action Secondary buttons, less important actions
--a-color-on-secondary Text ON secondary Text inside secondary-colored elements
--a-color-success Positive status Success messages, confirmations
--a-color-on-success Text ON success Text inside success-colored elements
--a-color-error Error status Error messages, destructive actions
--a-color-on-error Text ON error Text inside error-colored elements
--a-color-warning Warning status Warning messages, cautions
--a-color-on-warning Text ON warning Text inside warning-colored elements
--a-color-info Info status Informational messages, hints
--a-color-on-info Text ON info Text inside info-colored elements
--a-color-surface Primary background Page backgrounds, containers
--a-color-surface-variant Secondary background Hover states, subtle backgrounds
--a-color-on-surface Primary text Main body text
--a-color-on-surface-variant Secondary text Labels, helper text, muted text
--a-color-outline Borders & dividers Borders, lines, separators

How to Use in Your CSS

Always use design tokens, never hardcoded colors:

✅ Correct:
.button { background: var(--a-color-primary); }
❌ Incorrect:
.button { background: #2563eb; }

Real-world component example:

/* Alert component using semantic colors */
.alert {
    padding: var(--a-space-m);
    border-radius: var(--a-border-radius-medium);
}

.alert.success {
    background: var(--a-color-success-container);
    color: var(--a-color-on-success-container);
    border-left: 4px solid var(--a-color-success);
}

.alert.error {
    background: var(--a-color-error-container);
    color: var(--a-color-on-error-container);
    border-left: 4px solid var(--a-color-error);
}

Dark Mode Support

AXIOM01's color tokens automatically adapt for dark mode. Simply add data-theme="dark" to your HTML element and all colors adjust automatically.

<html data-theme="dark">
    <!-- Your content automatically uses dark mode colors -->
</html>

The same CSS works in both light and dark modes — tokens handle the adaptation!

[Ax01]
